title: Adaptive Faster Polling
goal: Capture denser drive paths and sharper charge curves by polling every ~20–30s only while the car is actively driving or charging — without leaving the read-only, no-virtual-key, Workers + Supabase architecture.

changelog.note: Initial draft — folds in roast findings: session-close hysteresis, connection discipline, DB compare-and-swap as the primary token guarantee, a consecutive-failure circuit breaker, and a cost pre-flight check.

loop.caption: Cron arms a per-vehicle burst poller only while active; hysteresis guards session close; idle hands back to the cron so the car sleeps.

nodes.label: Baseline cron
nodes.detail: Runs every 2 minutes. Lists vehicles, records state, and (as today) reads vehicle data for online cars to detect activity. Unchanged from current behaviour.

nodes.label: Arm burst poller
nodes.detail: One per vehicle. Armed only via the in-Worker binding from the scheduler — never a public route.

nodes.label: Burst poll
nodes.detail: Every 20s driving / 30s charging. Re-arms the next alarm BEFORE the fallible poll, then: read vehicle data → write snapshot → run the existing per-vehicle sessionization. Builds a fresh DB client per alarm with a tiny pool (1–2 conns), closed promptly.

nodes.label: Inactive N times?
nodes.detail: Hysteresis: require N CONSECUTIVE not-driving-and-not-charging reads before closing. Guards against transient shift_state null / charging 'Stopped' blips that dense 20s polling would otherwise split into many spurious sessions.

nodes.label: Close + stop
nodes.detail: Let the existing logic close the drive/charge session, then stop re-arming. Hard caps (max poll-count + consecutive failures) also stop the loop and fall back to the cron baseline.

context:
• Drive paths and charge curves are coarse because the poller samples every 2 minutes — turns render polygonal and the charge ramp/taper is under-sampled.
  • True 500ms Fleet Telemetry was separately evaluated and rejected (it needs a paired virtual key, an always-on mTLS server, and 100–1000× the write volume). This is the read-only-friendly middle ground.
  • Hard constraints: stay read-only (no virtual key, no commands) on Cloudflare Workers + Supabase; the platform cron floors at 1-minute granularity.
  • Battery safety: frequently polling an already-online parked car delays sleep → vampire drain. A sleeping car cannot be woken by a read (it returns 408), so the risk is the parked-online case, not the sleeper.
approachesConsidered:
ApproachGistTradeoffStatus
Active-burst poller + cron kickerCron stays the baseline; on detecting drive/charge it arms a per-vehicle burst poller that tight-polls 20s/30s, then stops.Adds a stateful component + new concurrency to reason about.✓ Picked — smallest steady-state change, bounded cost, preserves sleep, reuses the one sessionization path.
Burst poller owns all pollingMove every online poll into the stateful timer; cron just keeps it alive.Cleaner single path, but a bigger rewrite and the timer now owns idle + sleep tuning.Rejected — more surface to get exactly right for no extra fidelity.
1-minute cron onlyChange the schedule from 2 min to 1 min.Trivial, no new infra, but caps at 60s.Rejected — can't reach the 20–30s the goal needs.
In-invocation sleep loopOne cron tick that sleeps ~15s between polls.—Rejected — Workers clamp timers (they advance only after I/O), so sub-minute spacing silently fails in production.
approach:
The 2-minute cron stays the baseline and watchdog: it keeps detecting activity exactly as today. When it sees a vehicle driving or charging, it arms a per-vehicle burst poller that drives a self-re-arming ~20s (driving) / ~30s (charging) loop, running the same snapshot + sessionization step the cron already uses — so the denser samples flow straight through the existing density-agnostic aggregation. The loop re-arms before each fallible poll, debounces session close behind N consecutive inactive reads, and stops on a clean close or a safety cap — handing idle/asleep back to the cron so the car sleeps normally. The one genuinely new failure mode — two execution contexts touching the atomically-rotating refresh token — is contained by the database compare-and-swap on the token row (the only guard that spans isolates), not by best-effort scheduling.

{{diagram:loop}}
decisions:
DecisionPickedRejectedWhy
Sub-minute mechanismPer-vehicle burst poller, self-re-arming alarmIn-tick sleep loop; sub-minute cronOnly platform-reliable way to space polls under 60s — Workers clamp timers so a sleep loop silently fails in prod, and cron floors at 1 min.
Burst-poller scopeActive-burst only; cron stays baselineBurst poller owns all polling; 1-min cron onlySmallest steady-state change, bounded cost, preserves existing sleep behaviour, reuses one sessionization path.
Active cadence20s driving / 30s charging15s/20s; 30s/60sBest detail-per-dollar — smooth GPS paths without doubling Fleet-API calls; charge curves are smooth, so 30s suffices.
Detection baselineKeep the 2-minute cron1-minute cronZero idle-cost / sleep regression; the ≤2-min arming lag loses at most a drive's first minute or two.
Burst-poller identityOne per vehiclePer-user; singletonClean isolation, multi-vehicle-ready at no extra cost.
NEW Session-close debounceRequire N consecutive inactive reads before closingClose on the first inactive read (today's behaviour)At 20s, transient shift-state null / charging "Stopped" blips would split one drive/charge into many, corrupting per-drive Wh/mi and the new charge-cycle sums; sparse 2-min polling masked this.
NEW Stop + safety capsStop on debounced-inactive; re-arm before the fallible poll; caps on max poll-count AND consecutive failuresA single max-poll-count capA success cap doesn't stop a failure loop — platform retries + cron re-arm become a low-grade storm burning Fleet calls + DB connections; a consecutive-failure breaker falls back to the self-recovering cron baseline.
CHANGED Cross-context token safetyDB compare-and-swap on the token row is the PRIMARY guarantee; cron + reconcile deferral is best-effort onlyTreating "cron defers to burst poller" as the guaranteeIn-process single-flight doesn't span isolates, and the hourly reconcile also touches the token, so two-plus contexts can hit the rotating refresh token; only the DB CAS spans isolates.
NEW Connection disciplineFresh DB client per alarm, tiny pool (1–2), closed promptlyReuse the default pool per alarmA recurring 20s connection producer feeds the same fan-out that previously caused connection-exhaustion errors; keep its footprint minimal and confirm the connection pooler absorbs it alongside SSR + crons.
tradeoffs:
• ACCEPT: ≤2-min lag before the burst poller arms → a drive's first minute or two is captured at baseline resolution.
  • ACCEPT: ~1.5× Fleet-API calls during active windows → modest cost increase, gated to active periods only.
  • ACCEPT: session-close hysteresis runs a few extra polls past the true end before closing → negligible at 20s.
  • ACCEPT: one new stateful component (a per-vehicle burst poller) → small added complexity, but Workers-native and ~$0 incremental.
  • OUT: idle/asleep cadence is unchanged — no faster polling of parked cars (protects sleep).
  • OUT: charge curves are not the justification — 60s already captures them; the real win is drive-path smoothness.
  • OUT: true sub-second / 500ms telemetry — separately rejected (needs a virtual key + an always-on mTLS server).
openQuestions:
• What is the actual current Fleet-API monthly spend versus the $10 credit — and if it is already over, should the cadence start slower than 20s?
  • Does the token store, on a losing compare-and-swap, re-read the winner's freshly-rotated refresh token rather than reuse the stale one (must be verified during implementation)?
  • Should a future "sleep mode" back the idle cron down to a cheap state-only check for long-parked cars to offset the burst poller's added cost, accepting it might miss a charge-start?
  • Should the active cadence be a user-facing setting, or stay hardcoded to begin with?
security:
AreaDecision
User scopingBurst poller carries (vehicle, user); every read/write keeps the user filter — the RLS-lockdown model is unchanged.
Token raceDB compare-and-swap on the token row is the cross-isolate guarantee; cron + reconcile deferral reduces overlap but isn't relied on; forced re-auth on a rejected read as backstop.
Trigger surfaceBurst poller armed only via the in-Worker binding from the scheduler — no public kick route; if one is ever added it reuses the existing cron Bearer secret.
Secrets at restTesla tokens stay encrypted at rest in the database; burst-poller storage holds only scheduling state — never tokens or location/PII.
Runaway protectionHard caps on max poll-count and consecutive failures, plus re-arm-before-poll; the existing 6h stale-session reaper is the database backstop.
Battery safetyTight polling gated strictly to driving/charging; parked-online cars are never polled faster than today, so sleep is preserved.
Read-only postureN/A — no commands, no virtual key; the read-only Fleet-API scope is unchanged.
